column1: What is the primary difference between a Tokenizer and an Embedding Model?
column2: A tokenizer maps raw text to arbitrary integer IDs (no meaning), while an embedding model maps those IDs to dense vectors representing semantic meaning.

column1: Why is Cosine Similarity preferred over Euclidean Distance for semantic search in RAG applications?
column2: Cosine similarity measures the angle (direction) between vectors, ignoring their magnitude. This prevents long documents from being penalized when compared to short queries.

column1: What is the purpose of Mean Pooling in an NLP embedding pipeline?
column2: It averages the individual token embeddings of a sequence into a single, unified document-level vector representation.

column1: How does an Attention Mask function during the mean pooling process?
column2: It acts as a binary filter (1s and 0s) to ensure that padding tokens are ignored and do not skew the average of the document vector.

column1: In the context of Amazon Bedrock and Titan Text Embeddings V2, how can you reduce vector database storage costs by up to 96%?
column2: By utilizing Binary Embeddings, which compress float32 dimensions into 1-bit representations with minimal accuracy loss.

column1: What is the primary benefit of applying 4-bit quantization to a Large Language Model (LLM)?
column2: It drastically reduces the GPU VRAM required to load and run the model (e.g., reducing a 16GB footprint to ~4GB) with minimal loss in performance.

column1: What do the dimensions (e.g., 1536 or 4096) of an embedding vector represent?
column2: They represent latent semantic features learned by the model during training, defining the token's exact coordinates in a semantic meaning-space.

column1: Which AWS service natively uses cosine similarity by default to retrieve relevant document chunks for RAG architectures?
What is the purpose of using PCA and t-SNE on text embeddings?
column2: To perform dimensionality reduction, allowing high-dimensional semantic vectors to be visualized and clustered in 2D or 3D space.
